title: Support 'parameter type' in hierarchical blocks (#5309)
body:
This RFC PR introduces an intermediate file-based approach for implementing type parameter passing in hierarchical verilation.

Note: a huge part of the diff originates from trace tests due to running an additional type parameter flow (previously unsupported).

## Current implementation of simple parameter passing

Currently, Verilator supports passing simple parameters, such as number and string literals, between modules.
This is implemented by stringifying those parameters and appending them to `*hierMkArgs` of a hierarchical module.
These parameters are later used in the `V3Param` where, based on hashed parameters, relevant deparameterized hierarchical module instances are assigned to cells.

However, this approach would not be applicable to type parameters. As I mentioned previously in https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/5309,
handling type parameters with this stringifcation approach would introduce the need for reimplementing SystemVerilog parsing just for that.

## Proposed implementation of type parameter passing

I propose an intermediate file-based approach for passing type parameters to `hier_block` modules.
This approach introduces modifications to all 3 phases of hierarchical verilation.

### Phase 1 (Launched when `m_hierarchical` is set)
  In `V3HierBlock` for each `hier_block` generate:
    1. Generate `*__hierMkArgs`
    2. Generate `*__hierTypeParameters.sv` (if type parameters present)
        Example `*__hierTypeParameters.sv`:
        ```verilog
          module _V_type_parameters();
            typedef logic[8:0] PARAM;
          endmodule
        ```

### Phase 2 (Launched when `m_hierChild` is set)
  In `V3LinkDot`:
    1. Read `*__hierTypeParameters.sv` and insert them into `VSymEnt`.
    2. Replace each `AstParamTypeDType*` with type parameter inserted previously.

### Phase 3 (Launched when !`m_hierBlocks.empty()` && !`m_hierChild` holds)
  In `V3Param`:
    1. If type parameter file was generated, find instantiations of module cells by its type param value hashes.


Note: this implementation only works for `AstBasicDType` types. For more complex types, a rewrite of `V3Param` is required, but this is also the case for a stringification-based approach.

For simplicity's sake, the stringification-based implementation was not replaced by the intermediate file-based approach, but these two should be unified in the final implementation.

__Please take a look at the PoC implementation and evaluate whether it is a good approach for implementing this feature.__
